name: Uranium Sector
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **URA $58.26 (+7.5% 1D, +21.5% 1M, +6.1% 3M) — APPARENT ANOMALY THAT'S ACTUALLY CONSISTENT: BENEFITS FROM BOTH PEACE TRACK (HEU CUSTODIAN PATH UNDER MOU) AND STRUCTURAL AI POWER DEMAND.** The 14-point MOU explicitly requires Iran to ship out HEU stockpile to a third-party custodian (Russia rejected by US; potentially IAEA, Kazakhstan, or US-based facility). This is a NUCLEAR FUEL CYCLE LEGITIMIZATION signal — civilian uranium custody and processing infrastructure becomes more politically valuable. Plus: $2.7B 10-year US enrichment commitment continues; AI-data-center power demand continues; Cameco (24% of URA) up sharply. Uranium ETF already +117% TTM — supply-squeeze narrative undisturbed by oil-crash regime. Crisis-linked %: ~50% (HEU custodian path); ~50% structural (AI power demand). **URA does NOT reverse cleanly on either MOU path** — structural bull intact regardless of Iran response. The dual exposure is the rare beneficial structural setup. JPost specifically described MOU provision as 'Tehran give away enriched uranium' — custodian undefined, but nuclear-fuel-cycle infrastructure rerates regardless. Trump's 'bombing starts at significantly higher level and intensity' rhetorical leverage maintains tail-risk on Iran-nuclear-facility strike (RHETORIC/THREAT, not active).

active_triggers.condition: Confirmed US/Israeli kinetic action on Iran nuclear-enrichment facility (Natanz, Fordow, Arak)
URA breaks $62 sustained for 3 sessions (above 1M peak +25% from baseline)

URA +117% TTM — supply-squeeze narrative undisturbed by oil-crash regime. ~50% crisis (custodian) / ~50% structural (AI). Does NOT reverse cleanly on either MOU path.
